anuppannānaṁ.gram: past participle of na uppajjati, genitive plural

anuppattasadattho.meaning: having attained one's own welfare; one who has reached his own true goal
breakdown: Attained, reached (past participle of anupāpuṇāti; the pp is sandhi doubling).
One's own welfare, one's own good (sa(ka) + attha).
One's own welfare, one's own good.

anuppatto.gram: past participle of anupāpuṇāti, masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Root "to reach, obtain" + past participle suffix -ta.

anuppaveccheyya.gram: verb, 3rd person singular optative of anuppavecchati

anuppiyabhāṇī.meaning: a flatterer, a sycophant; one who speaks only what is pleasing

anuppādāya.meaning: for the non-arising (of unarisen unwholesome states)

anupubbena.meaning: in due course, gradually, in succession
breakdown: Former, preceding; anupubba = "one after the other".

anupubbiṁ.meaning: successive, gradual (as in anupubbiṁ kathaṁ, the gradual discourse)

anupādisesāya.meaning: without any substratum of becoming remaining; with no residue of clinging to existence left
anupādisesāya.summary: Used of the nibbāna element in which no fuel of existence remains (anupādisesa nibbānadhātu).
breakdown: Substratum of becoming, fuel of clinging.

anupādiyanto.gram: present participle of na upādiyati, masculine nominative singular

anupādāya.meaning: not holding on to; free from clinging

anusitthakāranaṁ.meaning: doing as instructed; acting according to the instruction

anusitthakāranaṁ doing as.note: uncertain: this key carries the English words "doing as" over from the source glossary's layout; the Pāli entry is anusitthakāranaṁ "doing as instructed".

anussaretha.meaning: you should remember, you should recollect
anussaretha.gram: verb, 2nd person plural optative of anussarati

anussareyyātha.meaning: you should recollect, you should remember
anussareyyātha.gram: verb, 2nd person plural optative of anussarati

anussaritā.meaning: remembered, followed up in memory; one who recollects
anussaritā.gram: past participle of anussarati (also agent noun anussaritā "a rememberer")

breakdown: Root "to hear"; anussava = hearsay, tradition received by ear.

anussavādivasena.meaning: on account of hearsay, tradition and so forth
anussavādivasena.gram: compound noun, instrumental singular used adverbially
breakdown: And so forth, et cetera (literally "beginning with").
On account of, by way of.

anussito.meaning: not erected, not hoisted; not haughty
anussito.gram: past participle of na usseti, masculine nominative singular

anusāsanti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present of anusāsati (anu + √sās)

anusāsi.gram: verb, 3rd person singular aorist of anusāsati

anusūyāyamāno.gram: present participle of na usūyati, masculine nominative singular

breakdown: Root "to burn, torment"; anutappati = "to feel remorse".

anuviccāpi.meaning: even after coming to know, also having found out

anuvicāreti.meaning: to think about, to ponder upon
anuvicāreti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, causative of anuvicarati
breakdown: Root "to move"; causative cāre- — "to make the mind range over, ponder".

anuyantesu.meaning: among subjects, among those who follow; applying themselves to
anuyantesu.gram: present participle of anuyāti, locative plural
breakdown: Root "to go"; anuyāti = "to follow, be subject to".

anuyogamanvāya.meaning: in consequence of application; as a result of devoted practice
breakdown: Undergoing, in consequence of (gerund of anveti). (The ṁ becomes m before a vowel.)

anuyogaṁ.meaning: application, practice; engaging in (the development of)
breakdown: Root "to yoke, engage"; anuyoga = application, devotion to practice.

anuyyutaṁ.gram: past participle of anu + √yu, accusative singular
breakdown: Root "to bind, join" + past participle suffix -ta.

anuññātaṁ.gram: past participle of anujānāti, neuter/accusative singular

anuṭṭhātā.meaning: one who does not get up; not rising to action, inactive
breakdown: One who rises, gets up to act (ud + √ṭhā + -tar).

anvāssaveyyuṁ.meaning: would flow in upon, would befall, would result
anvāssaveyyuṁ.gram: verb, 3rd person plural optative of anvāssavati
breakdown: Prefix "after, along" (becoming anv- before a vowel).

anvāya.meaning: undergoing, in consequence of, following upon

anāgata.gram: past participle of na āgacchati; as a noun, the future

anāgatamaddhānaṁ.meaning: in the future, in time to come
anāgatamaddhānaṁ.gram: adverbial compound, accusative singular (with junction consonant m)

anāgatappajappāya.meaning: longing for the future; hankering after what is yet to come
anāgatappajappāya.gram: feminine abstract compound noun, oblique singular
breakdown: Longing, hankering (pa + √japp; the pp is sandhi doubling).

anāgāmi.meaning: a non-returner; one who does not come back (to this world)
anāgāmi.summary: The third of the four stages of liberation: one who, having abandoned the five lower fetters, is not reborn in the sensual world.
anāgāmi.goenka: The anāgāmī, non-returner, stands at the third of the four stages of liberation recognised in the tradition — after the sotāpanna (stream-enterer) and sakadāgāmī (once-returner), and before the arahant. Having eradicated the lower fetters through the progressive purification of the mind, such a one is not born again in the sensual world but reaches final liberation from a higher plane.

anāgāmiphalaṁ.meaning: the fruit of non-return; attainment of the stage of a non-returner

anākulā.meaning: not entangled; untroubled, free from conflict

anālayo.meaning: free from attachment; without shelter or abode (for craving)

anāsavacittassa.meaning: of one whose mind is free from impurities (the āsavas)
